title: Quiet Form
description: A restrained structured starter generated from broad public engineering repositories, ideal for minimal and text-first developer portfolios.
sourceLabel: Inspired by real-world repositories from Google’s open-source ecosystem

theme.name: Signal Violet
theme.reason: High-contrast and expressive for community-forward personal brands.

previewSnapshot.summary: Google ❤️ Open Source The portfolio draft reflects focused most often on C++, Python, Java, and Go and based in United States of America, with selected work such as gemma.cpp, langextract, and latexify_py.

subheadline.value: Built from 6 featured repositories drawn from recent public GitHub work with a focus on C++, Python, and Java. This draft starts from real code, repository context, and README signals instead of placeholder portfolio copy.

description.value: Google ❤️ Open Source Based in United States of America. Selected repositories and README content are being used to shape the project story and technical focus.
Share the clearest way to connect for roles, freelance work, collaborations, or portfolio conversations.
Add the professional destinations you want visitors to open next, including your resume, cover letter, LinkedIn, Handshake, and portfolio links.

repositories.name: google/material-design-icons
repositories.description: Material Design icons by Google (Material Symbols)
repositories.readmeExcerpt: These are two different official icon sets from Google, using the same underlying designs. Material Symbols is the current set, introduced in April 2022, built on variable font technology. Material Icons is the classic set, but no longer updated. More details below.

repositories.name: google/guava
repositories.readmeExcerpt: Guava is a set of core Java libraries from Google that includes new collection types (such as multimap and multiset), immutable collections, a graph library, and utilities for concurrency, I/O, hashing, primitives, strings, and more! It is widely used on most Java projects within Google, and widely used by many other companies as well.

repositories.name: google/zx
repositories.description: A tool for writing better scripts
repositories.readmeExcerpt: Bash is great, but when it comes to writing more complex scripts, many people prefer a more convenient programming language. JavaScript is a perfect choice, but the Node.js standard library requires additional hassle before using. No compromise, take the best of both. The zx package provides useful cross-platform wrappers around child_process, escapes arguments and gives sensible defaults.

repositories.name: google/styleguide
repositories.description: Style guides for Google-originated open-source projects
repositories.readmeExcerpt: Every major open-source project has its own style guide: a set of conventions (sometimes arbitrary) about how to write code for that project. It is much easier to understand a large codebase when all the code in it is in a consistent style.

repositories.name: google/googletest
repositories.description: GoogleTest - Google Testing and Mocking Framework
repositories.readmeExcerpt: Our documentation is now live on GitHub Pages at https://google.github.io/googletest/. We recommend browsing the documentation on GitHub Pages rather than directly in the repository.

repositories.name: google/leveldb
repositories.description: LevelDB is a fast key-value storage library written at Google that provides an ordered mapping from string keys to string values.
repositories.readmeExcerpt: LevelDB is a fast key-value storage library written at Google that provides an ordered mapping from string keys to string values.

contact.title: Keep it easy to connect
